label: Oracle Database
description: Connect to an Oracle Database instance using host, port, service name or SID, and credentials.

form.title: Connect Oracle Database
form.subtitle: Provide your Oracle host, port, service name or SID, and login credentials.

methods.label: Service Name (recommended)
methods.description: Connect using the Oracle service name — the modern, preferred identifier for most Oracle 12c+ databases, RAC clusters, and cloud-hosted instances.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Oracle Service Name

The service name is the standard way to identify an Oracle database in modern deployments (Oracle 12c and later, RAC, Oracle Autonomous DB, etc.).

### Steps

1. Ask your DBA or check your `tnsnames.ora` file for the **service name**. It looks like `ORCL`, `mydb.example.com`, or a long string like `abcxyz_high` for Autonomous Database.
2. Confirm the **hostname or IP address** of the Oracle listener (e.g., `db.example.com`).
3. The default listener port is **1521** — verify with your DBA if it differs.
4. Enter a database user with the appropriate read/write privileges. The account must be able to log in from the host running Anton.
5. Paste all values into the fields below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- Service names are preferred over SIDs for Oracle 12c+ and all cloud deployments.
- If your database uses Oracle Wallet or mTLS (e.g., Autonomous Database), use the **TNS Connection String** method instead and supply the wallet path in your environment.
- Ensure the Oracle listener port is open in any firewall between Anton and the database server.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Host
fields.description: Hostname or IP address of the Oracle listener.

fields.name: service_name
fields.label: Service Name
fields.description: The Oracle service name (not SID). Check tnsnames.ora or ask your DBA.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Oracle database user with appropriate privileges.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the Oracle database user. Stored securely in Anton's local vault.

methods.label: SID (Legacy)
methods.description: Connect using the Oracle System Identifier (SID) — the older, instance-level identifier. Use this for legacy Oracle 11g databases or on-premise instances where a service name is not configured.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Oracle SID

The SID (System Identifier) is the legacy way to address a specific Oracle instance. Use this only if your database doesn't expose a service name.

### Steps

1. Find your SID — commonly `ORCL`, `XE`, or a short custom string. Check `tnsnames.ora` or ask your DBA.
2. Confirm the **hostname or IP address** of the Oracle listener.
3. The default port is **1521** — confirm with your DBA.
4. Enter the Oracle user credentials.
5. Fill in all fields below and click **Submit**.

### Notes

- SID-based connections are deprecated in Oracle 12c+ in favor of service names.
- If the connection is refused, the listener may be configured for service-name routing only — try the **Service Name** method instead.

fields.name: host

fields.label: SID
fields.description: The Oracle System Identifier for the target instance (e.g., ORCL, XE).

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Oracle database user with appropriate privileges.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the Oracle database user. Stored securely in Anton's local vault.

methods.label: TNS Connection String
methods.description: Paste a full TNS connect descriptor or Easy Connect Plus string. Useful for complex configurations like Oracle RAC, Autonomous Database with wallet, or multi-host setups.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via TNS Connection String

Use this method when you have a full TNS descriptor (from `tnsnames.ora`) or an Easy Connect Plus string, particularly for Oracle Autonomous Database, RAC, or failover setups.

### Steps

1. Locate your connection string from one of:
   - Your `tnsnames.ora` file (the block inside parentheses after the alias)
   - Oracle Cloud Console → Database → DB Connection → Download client credentials, then check `tnsnames.ora` inside the wallet zip
   - Your DBA
2. Example Easy Connect Plus string: `myhost:1521/myservice` or `(DESCRIPTION=(ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=TCP)(HOST=myhost)(PORT=1521))(CONNECT_DATA=(SERVICE_NAME=myservice)))`
3. For Autonomous Database, also set the `TNS_ADMIN` environment variable on the machine running Anton to point to the unzipped wallet directory (Anton will pick this up automatically).
4. Paste the full string into the **TNS Connection String** field below, along with your credentials.

### Notes

- Do not include the username or password inside the connection string — enter them in the dedicated fields.
- If using mTLS/wallet, the wallet files must be accessible on the filesystem of the Anton host.

fields.name: tns_connection_string
fields.label: TNS Connection String
fields.description: Full TNS descriptor or Easy Connect Plus string. Do not include username/password here.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Oracle database user with appropriate privileges.

fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the Oracle database user. Stored securely in Anton's local vault.
